beats.activity: Tend to rooftop garden beds and water plants
beats.reasoning: Sunny Monday is perfect for urban gardening; she capitalizes on the weather and her morning energy before the day gets busy.

beats.reasoning: 11am match kickoff; she watches from the station during lunch rather than going out (risk-averse, routine-bound, and she's on shift). Eats at station to stay available.

beats.location_name: Capitol Hill Fire Station (Capitol Hill, Seattle)

beats.location_name: Capitol Hill Fire Station (Capitol Hill, Seattle)

beats.activity: Commute home from Capitol Hill Fire Station via bus and walking
beats.reasoning: 24-hour shift ends; she heads home to Beacon Hill after the overnight work, avoiding any remaining Light Rail closure impacts.

diary: Started the day right with my usual pour-over ritual on the rooftop—the tomato plants are finally coming in, and watering them this morning felt like the calm before the storm. Knew it'd be a long 24-hour shift, so I savored it. The commute to Capitol Hill was smooth, caught the tail end of the USA-Wales match during lunch with the crew. Everyone was invested, even though we only had maybe thirty minutes to watch before calls started rolling in. Had a couple of structure fires and a medical assist that kept us moving through the afternoon and into the evening. Nothing catastrophic, but enough to keep the adrenaline steady. Grabbed dinner with the team around dusk—someone made pasta, which helped. By the time I headed home after midnight, my legs were tired but my mind was still buzzing. Collapsed into bed knowing I did good work today.
thought_process: The beautiful weather set a good tone early on, and tending to my garden grounded me before the intensity of the shift. I almost felt guilty enjoying that quiet morning knowing what a 24-hour rotation typically holds. The World Cup match was a nice mental break during lunch—it gave the station a moment of levity, even though we all knew we'd be interrupted. The calls came steady but manageable, and I found myself grateful for a crew that moves well together. By evening, I was running on fumes but satisfied. The commute home felt longer than usual; my body was ready to shut down, and I think I'm still processing the minor stress of a few close calls today. Nothing traumatic, but enough to remind me why I do this.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
